data.title: NORWOOD FINANCIAL CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Norwood Financial Corp, a Pennsylvania-based holding company for Wayne Bank, has released its 2024 10-K report, detailing significant financial and operational performance metrics, strategic initiatives, and the challenges faced during the year. The company, which operates as an independent community bank, provides a range of personal and business credit services, trust and investment products, and real estate settlement services across northeastern Pennsylvania and parts of New York.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $112.58 million, reflecting an increase from $95.54 million in 2023 due to higher interest rates and increased loan balances.
  • Net Interest Income: $62.19 million, a slight increase from $62.07 million in 2023, despite a higher cost of funds.
  • Net (Loss) Income: $(0.16) million, a significant decrease from $16.76 million in 2023, primarily due to realized losses on securities sales.
  • (Loss) Earnings Per Share - Basic: $(0.02), compared to $2.08 in 2023, reflecting the net loss for the year.
  • (Loss) Earnings Per Share - Diluted: $(0.02), compared to $2.07 in 2023, consistent with the basic earnings per share.

Business Highlights

  • Company Overview: Norwood Financial Corp, through Wayne Bank, operates 30 offices serving northeastern Pennsylvania and parts of New York. The bank focuses on personal and business credit services, trust and investment products, and real estate settlement services.
  • Geographical Performance: Wayne Bank holds significant market shares in various counties, including the largest share of FDIC-insured deposits in Delaware County, New York, with 32.0%, and the fourth largest share in Sullivan County, New York, with 9.53%. In Pennsylvania, it ranks third in Wayne County with 21.54% and second in Pike County with 18.85%.
  • Loan Portfolio: As of December 31, 2024, the bank's loans receivable increased by $110.2 million, driven by a $43.5 million increase in consumer loans and a $41.7 million increase in commercial real estate loans.
  • Trust Activities: The bank's Wealth Management/Trust Department managed $205.1 million in assets as of December 31, 2024, reflecting growth and improved market valuations.
  • Subsidiary Activities: Norwood Investment Corp., a wholly owned subsidiary, focuses on annuity and mutual fund sales, generating $407,000 in gross revenues in 2024.
  • Operational Highlights: The bank operates 30 automated teller machines and offers a range of digital banking services, including internet banking, mobile banking, and remote deposit capture.
  • Future Outlook: The company aims to enhance profitability and liquidity through strategic repositioning of its investment portfolio and strengthening its capital position to support future growth.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Repositioning: In December 2024, the company undertook a strategic repositioning of its available-for-sale debt securities portfolio, selling securities with an amortized cost basis of approximately $175 million and purchasing new debt securities with a higher annual yield of 5.17%.
  • Capital Management: The company increased its stockholders' equity to $213.5 million as of December 31, 2024, from $181.1 million in 2023, primarily due to the net proceeds from a public offering of 1,150,000 shares of common stock, resulting in net proceeds of approximately $28 million.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue focusing on enhancing its capital structure and liquidity through strategic asset management and capital raising activities. The repositioning of the securities portfolio and the public offering are expected to support future growth and profitability.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Competition: The company operates in a highly competitive market, facing competition from other insured financial institutions, online banks, and investment products, which could impact its ability to attract and retain customers.
  • Investment and Market Volatility: The company experienced a net loss in 2024, primarily due to significant realized losses from the repositioning of its securities portfolio, highlighting the risk associated with investment decisions and market volatility.
  • Interest Rate Risk: Changes in interest rates have led to higher interest expenses, impacting net interest income and profitability. A 200-basis point increase in rates could decrease net interest income by 5.1% in the first year.
  • Operational Risks: The company faces operational risks from increased salaries, benefits, and data processing costs, which have risen significantly.
  • Credit Risk: The company remains exposed to credit risk, particularly in its commercial real estate and consumer loan portfolios, and must continue to manage credit quality to prevent future losses.
  • Reinvestment Risk: Changes in interest rates can affect the average life of loans and securities, potentially leading to lower yields on new investments.

SEC Filing: NORWOOD FINANCIAL CORP [ NWFL ] - 10-K - Mar. 14, 2025

data.title: Barclays cuts 2025 Brent price forecast on soft demand view
data.description:
Barclays on Friday lowered its 2025 Brent oil price forecast by $9 per barrel to $74 per barrel, citing a softer demand outlook amid elevated economic uncertainty.

"We turn neutral on oil prices relative to the curve and consensus, as we revise down our 2025 demand outlook 510 kb/d, due to soft high​-​frequency indicators and elevated economic uncertainty," analysts at Barclays said in a note.

data.title: ALERUS FINANCIAL CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Alerus Financial Corporation, a diversified financial services company headquartered in Grand Forks, North Dakota, has released its annual Form 10-K report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2024. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment. Alerus Financial offers a range of services including banking, retirement and benefit services, and wealth management through its subsidiary, Alerus Financial, National Association.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $222.0 million, driven by a $34.7 million increase in noninterest income and a $19.2 million increase in net interest income.
  • Net Income: $17.8 million, an increase of $6.1 million or 52.0% compared to the previous year, primarily due to increased noninterest income and net interest income.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.83, compared to $0.58 in the previous year, reflecting improved profitability.
  • Net Interest Income: $107.0 million, an increase of $19.2 million or 21.9% from the previous year, due to higher earning assets and organic loan growth.
  • Noninterest Income: $114.9 million, a 43.3% increase from the previous year, primarily due to a strategic balance sheet repositioning and increased wealth revenue.

Business Highlights

  • Company Overview: Alerus Financial Corporation is a diversified financial services company headquartered in Grand Forks, North Dakota, offering banking, retirement and benefit services, and wealth management through its subsidiary, Alerus Financial, National Association.
  • Business Segments: The company operates through three main business segments: banking, retirement and benefit services, and wealth management. The mortgage division was integrated into the banking division as of January 1, 2024.
  • Geographical Performance: The primary banking market areas include North Dakota, Minnesota (Twin Cities MSA and Rochester MSA), and Arizona (Phoenix MSA). The company also administers retirement and benefit services plans nationwide through offices in Michigan, Minnesota, and Colorado.
  • Revenue Segments: Noninterest income is primarily driven by the retirement and benefit services and wealth business lines, with significant contributions from transaction or participant-based fees and market value-based fees.
  • Client-Centric Model: The company emphasizes a client-centric business model, focusing on offering diversified solutions through a relationship-oriented approach, targeting specific business and consumer segments with growth potential.
  • One Alerus Initiative: Launched in 2017, this initiative aims to integrate all product and service offerings cohesively, combining technology and skilled advisors to enhance client engagement and service delivery.
  • Technology Integration: The 'My Alerus' client access portal provides a unified system for clients to view and manage multiple financial products with a single login, enhancing the client experience and engagement.
  • Recent Acquisitions: The company expanded its business through the acquisition of MPB BHC, Inc. in 2022 and HMN Financial, Inc. in 2024, enhancing its presence in the Phoenix MSA and Rochester MSA, respectively.
  • Human Capital Resources: As of December 31, 2024, the company employed 892 individuals, with a focus on attracting and retaining top talent to support its client-centric and diversified business model.
  • Future Outlook: The company aims to leverage its existing client base for future organic growth and continue providing strong returns to stockholders through its diversified business model and client-first philosophy.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Mortgage Division Integration: The company integrated its mortgage division into the banking division as of January 1, 2024, to streamline operations and reflect current management practices. This strategic move is aimed at enhancing operational efficiency and aligning the business structure with the company's strategic vision.
  • Talent Acquisition and Expansion: The company has invested in talent acquisition to support organic loan growth and added an equipment finance team in 2024, indicating a focus on expanding its service offerings and market reach.
  • Capital Management: The company reported a significant increase in total stockholders' equity, rising from $369.1 million in 2023 to $495.4 million in 2024, reflecting strong capital management practices. The company declared dividends of $0.79 per share in 2024, up from $0.75 per share in 2023, demonstrating a commitment to returning value to shareholders.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its focus on strategic acquisitions and organic growth to enhance its market position. The integration of the mortgage division into the banking division is expected to yield operational efficiencies and better align the company's resources with its strategic goals. Additionally, the company aims to leverage its diversified business model to drive revenue growth, with a particular emphasis on expanding its retirement and benefit services and wealth management segments. The company is also committed to maintaining strong capital ratios and exploring opportunities for further capital investments to support sustainable growth.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market and Interest Rate Risks: The company faces significant market and interest rate risks, particularly due to fluctuations in interest rates that may adversely affect earnings and demand for products. The Federal Open Market Committee's actions on interest rates could impact the national economy and the company's net interest income. Additionally, the company could recognize losses on securities if interest rates increase or economic conditions deteriorate.
  • Credit Risks: Credit risks are heightened by the company's concentration of large loans to certain borrowers and a significant portion of its loan portfolio being comprised of commercial real estate loans, which are sensitive to real estate market conditions. Nonperforming assets also pose a risk, requiring significant time and resources to resolve.
  • Operational Risks: Operational risks include the company's reliance on fee-based services, which are susceptible to economic and market conditions. The company also faces challenges in implementing growth strategies, both organic and through acquisitions, and in managing cybersecurity threats, including those involving artificial intelligence.
  • Liquidity and Funding Risks: Liquidity and funding risks are present due to the company's need to manage liquidity risk and dependence on dividends from the bank.
  • Legal, Accounting, and Compliance Risks: Legal, accounting, and compliance risks include the effectiveness of the company's risk management framework and potential regulatory changes.
  • Management Strategies: Management has identified the need to manage interest rate risks and credit risks as critical to maintaining financial stability. Strategies include maintaining disciplined underwriting standards and ensuring adequate allowance for credit losses. The company is also focused on enhancing its technological capabilities to address operational risks and improve efficiency.
  • Market Risk Management: The company is exposed to market risks related to interest rate fluctuations, which could impact the value of its securities portfolio and net interest income. Management is monitoring these risks closely and adjusting strategies to mitigate potential adverse effects.

SEC Filing: ALERUS FINANCIAL CORP [ ALRS ] - 10-K - Mar. 14, 2025

data.title: Coda Octopus Group Inc expected to post earnings of 3 cents a share - Earnings Preview

data.title: First Bancorp Announces Cash Dividend
data.description:
First Bancorp Announces Cash Dividend

PR Newswire

SOUTHERN PINES, N.C., March 14, 2025

SOUTHERN PINES, N.C., March 14, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — The Board of Directors of First Bancorp (the "Company"), the parent company of First Bank, has declared a cash dividend on its common stock of $0.22 per share payable on April 25, 2025 to shareholders of record as of March 31, 2025.

Richard Moore, Chief Executive Officer of First Bancorp, stated, "Our Company had a strong fourth quarter performance with meaningful increases in adjusted net income and adjusted EPS while maintaining strong capital, liquidity and credit quality. We are pleased to continue distributing cash dividends to our shareholders, contributing to a valuable return on their investments."

First Bancorp is a bank holding company headquartered in Southern Pines, North Carolina, with total assets of $12.1 billion. Its principal activity is the ownership and operation of First Bank, a state-chartered community bank that operates 113 branches in North Carolina and South Carolina. Since 1935, First Bank has taken a tailored approach to banking, combining best-in-class financial solutions, helpful local expertise, and technology to manage a home or business. First Bank also provides SBA loans to customers through its nationwide network of lenders.

Please visit our website at www.LocalFirstBank.com for more information.

First Bancorp's common stock is traded on The NASDAQ Global Select Market under the symbol "FBNC." Member FDIC, Equal Housing Lender.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/first-bancorp-announces-cash-dividend-302398748.html

SOURCE First Bancorp

/CONTACT: Hillary Kestler, 704-644-4137

data.title: ACG Group Leads €12.5M Note Issuance for UK Digital Media Reacquisition Financing
data.description:
ACG Group's €300M note programme proves its versatility with €12.5M issuance, attracting prestigious institutional investors

LONDON, UK / ACCESS Newswire / March 14, 2025 / ACG Group has successfully arranged the second issuance under its €300 million CreditHarbor Note Programme. This €12.5 million note issuance is dedicated to the reacquisition financing of a company in the digital media sector.

Key features of the EMTN notes include a 12.5% yield, 5-year maturity, fixed coupon, and quarterly principal amortization of 3.125%. The notes are both Euroclear and Clearstream eligible.

Alberto Ferro-Villani, CEO at ACG Group, stated: "This second issuance under our CreditHarbor Programme showcases ACG's continued success in delivering tailored financing solutions. Our expertise in structuring complex deals, particularly in the digital media sector, reinforces our position as a versatile financial partner."

Legal support for the transaction was provided by DWF's team, led by Partner Luca Lo Po.

The ACG Group team is comprised of Alberto Ferro Villani, Myriam Zandonini, and Luca Crenna.

This transaction further demonstrates ACG's commitment to innovative financial structuring and its ability to meet diverse financing needs in the acquisition market. The issuance has been subscribed by a pool of international banks, including Edmond de Rothschild Bank, further validating the attractiveness of ACG's financial offerings to prestigious institutional investors.

This press release does not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy, nor shall there be any sale of these securities in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation, or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of such jurisdiction.

About ACG Group:

ACG Group is a full-service global investment group providing a comprehensive suite of investment, custody, and global market access services. ACG offers investors access to sophisticated active investment strategies, global investment opportunities outside the traditional banking offering, and innovative capital markets solutions. The Group is dedicated to becoming the partner of choice for investors, creating long-term relationships built on trust and integrity. For more information, please visit  https://acg.group/.

Press Contact:

ACG Group

442035405150

Colin.Lozzi@acg.group

https://acg.group/

SOURCE: ACG Group

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Banks are feeling the strain as lower-income consumers struggle with inflation and tariff chaos
data.description:
By Steve Gelsi

A JPMorgan analyst breaks down comments from banks about 2025, as financial cushions flatten for lower-end consumers

JPMorgan Chase banking analyst Vivek Juneja said Friday that banks could feel the crunch from U.S. consumers - especially lower-income consumers - as the first quarter of 2025 wraps up in the next couple of weeks, while overall credit strength remains robust for the sector.

"Consumers financial positioning especially at the lower end is being eroded by the impact of cumulative inflation with threat of further erosion with impact of tariffs and policies as well as layoffs," Juneja said in a research note. "The cushion (deposits less spending) is less than prepandemic levels for lower income and/or mass market segment - this will limit their ability to withstand a slowdown."

His summary of bank comments issued in recent weeks provided a glimpse of the economic conditions facing the financial sector as it wraps up the first quarter.

The big banks will issue profit updates, starting on April 11 for JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM), Wells Fargo & Co. (WFC) and Morgan Stanley (MS); April 14 for Goldman Sachs Group Inc. (GS); and April 15 for Citigroup Inc. (C) and Bank of America Corp. (BAC)

Citing comments on some cracks in the U.S. consumer by Walmart Inc. (WMT) and others in recent weeks, Juneja said spending data has been mixed.

Credit-card spending among Chase customers slowed to about 2.6% growth in February, from 3.7% to 4% between Nov. 1 and Jan. 30, but it's too early to see how those trends continued in March.

Spending has shifted to the wealthier consumers, which remains relatively healthy.

Delinquencies are rising on government student loans, and Federal Housing Administration 30-89 day delinquencies on mortgages are up sharply to 9% - well above the 2012-19 levels of 5% to 7.6%.

Credit-card delinquencies have been " flattish" recently and are highest at U.S. Bancorp (USB), Wells Fargo, and Citi due to those banks' large exposure to private-label cards, he said.

"Delinquencies seem to be flattening and increase in net charge-offs has been slowing but higher tariffs and layoffs could change this," Juneja said.

Overall, bank stocks have sold off about 15% from their recent peak in the face of jitters about an economic slowdown, as well as uncertainty about policies and their implications, he said.

This is all driving a drop in consumer confidence.

"The pendulum had swung sharply in favor of banks post-elections and now has swung very sharply the other way as these sentiments tend to go in extremes," Juneja said. "Clarity on policies and signs of meaningful reduction in cumulative inflation in essentials (food, healthcare, etc.) will be needed to restore consumer confidence, not just couple of additional rate cuts."

Even with the selloff, however, money-center banks are trading at 1.5 times the price to total book value on average. This is "a tad" above the long-term average of 1.3 times, he said.

-Steve Gelsi

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Polyrizon Signs Letter of Intent With Biotech Firm for Use of Its Intranasal Drug Delivery Platform
data.description:
Polyrizon said Friday that it has signed a non-binding letter of intent with an unidentified company specializing in psychedelic-based therapies to develop a novel intranasal formulation using Polyrizon's proprietary platform to improve the delivery of psychedelic treatments.

Polyrizon said it will develop the formulation using its mucoadhesive technology, while the psychedelic-focused company will fund the research and development, including feasibility studies.

After establishing proof of concept, both parties intend to finalize a definitive agreement on intellectual property, royalties, commercialization rights, and financial terms, Polyrizon said.

Shares of the company were down nearly 20% in recent premarket activity on Friday.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 9 AM ET
data.description:
Gold Exceeds $3,000, With Plenty of Room to Climb Further

Gold has exceeded $3,000 a troy ounce for the first time ever on economic uncertainty and safe-haven demand.

Stock Futures Gain After Shutdown Averted

With the threat of a government shutdown receding, stocks look set to end a tough week with a modest rally.

Here's Where to Look for Early Signs of a Recession

We already know what the jobs report tells us. But what about other indicators-like sales of mini liquor bottles?

Russia Calls for Trump-Putin Talks on Ukraine War

The Kremlin said there are reasons to be optimistic about a settlement to the conflict but that more work needs to be done.

Big Pharma Walked Away From Mental Health. Why Some Are Coming Back.

Psychiatry was once big business for pharma, with drugs like Prozac making billions. Now a few breakthroughs are reviving interest.

U.K. Economy Unexpectedly Shrinks, Posing New Challenge to Government

The contraction in the first month of the year is the latest frustration for a relatively new government that has pledged to bring an end to a decade-and-a-half of stagnation.

Chinese Shares Surge on Hopes for RRR Cut, Consumption Boosting Policies

Chinese shares closed at their highest level this year on Friday amid hopes that Beijing will soon move to shore up the economy.

Japan Wage Talks Signal Another Year of Big Pay Increases

Companies are again offering Japanese workers the biggest pay increase in three decades, the nation's largest labor union group said.

How Wall Street and Business Got Trump Wrong

They thought his second term would be like the first, giving priority to economic growth and the stock market. Trump had other ideas.

Trump Ramps Up EU Trade War With Tariff Threat on Champagne, Wine

President Trump said he would impose a 200% tariff on U.S. imports of wine, Champagne and other alcoholic beverages from the bloc.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 9 AM ET
data.description:
Tesla Develops Cheaper Model Y in China to Battle Growing Competition

The stripped-down SUV is aimed at recovering market share from Chinese makers offering less-expensive models.

Li Auto Squeezed by Price War as Profit Drops Despite Record Revenue

Chinese plug-in hybrid specialist Li Auto reported lower net profit for the fourth quarter despite record revenue as a brutal price war hurt its bottom line.

UniCredit Gets ECB Green Light to Raise Commerzbank Stake

The Italian bank said it will wait until next year before deciding whether to make a move after receiving European Central Bank authorization to increase its holding in Commerzbank to up to 29.9%.

BMW Expects $1.1 Billion Earnings Hit From Tariffs This Year

The automaker expects earnings to remain flat this year while sales will grow only slightly as a result of global trade tensions and a demand shift in China.

Foxconn Forecasts Strong 2025 Growth on AI Server Demand

Foxconn has expanded its footprint into high-growth tech sectors like AI and EVs in recent years as its core electronics manufacturing business faces challenges due to industry shifts.

CK Hutchison Shares Drop After Beijing Signals Unhappiness Over Panama Ports Deal

Beijing signaled its displeasure over CK Hutchison Holdings' agreement to sell its majority controlling stake in Panama ports to a BlackRock-led consortium.

Universal Music Shares Fall After Ackman's Pershing Decides to Cut Stake

Hedge fund manager Bill Ackman said his Pershing Square firm would reduce its stake in the world's largest music company.

Daimler Truck to Cut Costs at Mercedes-Benz Trucks Unit After Earnings Decline

The trucks and buses manufacturer said it aims to achieve more than EUR1 billion in savings after posting a decline in fourth-quarter earnings on weak European demand.

Inside the Mind of Intel's New CEO: 'Disrupt and Leapfrog'

Semiconductor veteran and former board member Lip-Bu Tan has returned to the company in a time of great need. The path ahead is rocky.

Compass in Talks to Buy Warren Buffett's Real-Estate Brokerage Unit

The talks are the latest sign of consolidation among real-estate brokerages during a prolonged period of lackluster home sales.

data.title: Ibotta Selects Board Member Sheppard for Interim CFO Role
data.description:
By Denny Jacob

Ibotta said board member Valarie Sheppard will serve as interim chief financial officer.

The personnel change at the performance-marketing platform comes as Sunit Patel departs the company as CFO to pursue an external opportunity. He will stay engaged with Ibotta in an advisory role for at least one year after the transition, the company said.

Sheppard joined Ibotta's board in 2021. Her past experience includes a stint at Procter & Gamble, where she held the role of treasurer and controller.

Ibotta has engaged a search firm for a permanent CFO.

Write to Denny Jacob at denny.jacob@wsj.com

data.title: Healthcare Integrated Technologies Inc. Successfully Raises $10 million in Friends & Family Funding
data.description:
Knoxville, TN, March 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Healthcare Integrated Technologies Inc. (OTC PINK: HITC), a global leader in AI-driven safety and monitoring solutions, proudly announces the successful completion of a $10 million capital raise as part of its strategic growth initiatives, including its ongoing efforts to meet the listing requirements for the NYSE American stock exchange.

The capital raise strengthens the Company’s financial position and supports its ongoing expansion efforts. The Company has been diligently working towards meeting the requirements for an uplisting to the NYSE American, including the minimum $2.00 per share stock price under the Board’s Standard 3 requirement.

Mr. Scott Boruff, CEO of Healthcare Integrated Technologies Inc. stated: “This funding is a significant step forward as we continue to execute on our growth strategy and work toward a potential uplisting to the NYSE American. An uplisting would enhance shareholder value by increasing visibility, improving liquidity and expanding access to the capital markets via institutional investors.”

The Company will continue providing updates as it progresses toward meeting all necessary requirements for an NYSE American listing.

About Healthcare Integrated Technologies Inc.

Healthcare Integrated Technologies Inc. is a publicly traded, fully reporting audited company specializing in ambient AI technology solutions, dedicated to enhancing safety and security across various sectors, including healthcare, education, transportation, manufacturing, incarceration, and commercial industries. The Company’s mission is to help save lives worldwide by developing advanced AI technologies designed to enhance situational awareness and mitigate risks in critical environments. The Company is currently marketing five products and solutions, including its initial product, SafeSpace® Fall Monitoring, which utilizes advanced AI monitoring tools to enhance resident safety in senior living, reduce the risk of injuries, and improve overall care efficiency. Additionally, the Company has expanded its services and offerings beyond senior living facilities, into schools and transportation where its has recently launched several innovative solutions.

For more information, visit www.gethitc.com or contact HITC’s media team at contact@gethitc.com.

Media Contact:

Justin Freishtat

Healthcare Integrated Technologies Inc.

Email: justin.freishtat@gethitc.com

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains certain forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. All forward-looking statements are subject to uncertainty and may not come to fruition. Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on any forward-looking statement including statements that list numbers and dates.

###

data.title: Greif Named 2025 Gallup Exceptional Workplace Award Winner
data.description:
DELAWARE, Ohio, March 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Greif, Inc. (NYSE: GEF, GEF.B), a global leader in industrial packaging products and services, announced today it has been recognized as a recipient of the Gallup Exceptional Workplace Award (GEWA) for having a best-in-class workplace. This is the second year in a row Greif has received this recognition.

“As a global organization, we are honored Gallup continues to recognize our highly engaged teams and performance-focused culture,” said Ole Rosgaard, President and CEO of Greif. “I am grateful to our more than 14,000 colleagues around the world for consistently demonstrating what it means to prioritize both people and performance.”

Gallup’s analysis of team engagement and performance is the most comprehensive workplace study ever conducted, with data on more than 3.3 million employees in 347 organizations across 53 industries and 90 countries. Highly engaged organizations significantly outperform their peers on important business outcomes, including customer ratings, profitability, productivity, turnover, safety incidents, shrinkage, absenteeism, quality, wellbeing and organizational citizenship.

“We are extremely honored to be recognized by Gallup yet again,” said Bala V. Sathyanarayanan, Greif's Executive Vice President and Chief Human Resources Officer. “Colleague engagement strengthens safety, improves customer service and enhances financial performance. It is fundamental to maintaining an exceptional company culture. We are pleased to be recognized for this important work.”

For more on Greif and its commitment to colleague engagement, visit https://www.greif.com/about-2/.

About Greif, Inc.

Greif is a global leader in industrial packaging products and services and is pursuing its vision: being the best customer service company in the world. The Company produces steel, plastic and fiber drums, intermediate bulk containers, reconditioned containers, jerrycans and other small plastics, containerboard, corrugated sheets and products, uncoated recycled paperboard, coated recycled paperboard, tubes and cores and a diverse mix of specialty products. The Company also manufactures packaging accessories and provides other services for a wide range of industries. In addition, Greif manages timber properties in the southeastern United States. The Company has a workforce of over 14,000 colleagues spread across more than 250 facilities in 37 countries to serve global as well as regional customers. Additional information is on the Company's website at www.greif.com.

Contact:

TJ Struhs

Director, Corporate Communications

207-956-2304 / tj.struhs@greif.com

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0700 ET - Gold futures rise to a record high on high levels of political uncertainty and optimism for U.S. Federal Reserve interest-rate cuts. Futures are up 0.4% at $3,004.0 a troy ounce, having reached a record high of $3,017.10/oz earlier in the session. Gold jumped after February's U.S. inflation figures showed weaker-than-expected price pressures, Commerzbank analysts say in a note. This has boosted hopes for the Fed to resume interest-rate cuts, given rising economic risks. However, investors should be cautious about jumping on the bandwagon too soon, Commerzbank says. The economic effects of U.S. tariff policies are in focus but inflation risks are just as significant, and there is no guarantee the Fed will balance both risks. The Fed is unlikely to completely ignore tariff effects and rate cuts will likely come later than market expectations, analysts add. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0610 ET - Palm oil ended higher, tracking gains in soybean oil on the Chicago Board of Trade and palm olein strength on the Dalian Commodity Exchange, said David Ng, a trader at Kuala Lumpur-based Iceberg X. Market sentiment was also lifted by concerns over weak production growth in the coming weeks, he added. Ng pegs support for CPO futures at 4,450 ringgit and resistance at 4,680 ringgit. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery closed 39 ringgit higher at 4,578 ringgit/ton. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0510 ET - Oil prices rise as hopes of an imminent end to the war in Ukraine fade after Russian President Vladimir Putin rejected an immediate cease-fire. Brent crude gains 1.2% at $70.74 a barrel, while WTI rises 1.4% to $67.47 a barrel. However, recession fears fueled by global trade tensions continue to cloud the demand outlook, with U.S. President Trump threatening to impose 200% tariffs on alcohol imports from the EU. "The ongoing trade war is expected to weigh on prices in the coming months, potentially complicating the Federal Reserve's ability to cut interest rates if economic conditions worsen," Sucden Financial's Daria Efanova and Viktoria Kuszak say in a note. Meanwhile, concerns over an oversupplied market linger as OPEC+ prepares to raise output next month and the IEA warns that global supplies could exceed demand by around 600,000 barrels a day. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0448 ET - Gold futures rise to a new record high on U.S. inflation data and safe-haven demand. Futures are up 0.4% at $3,003.40 a troy ounce, having risen as high as $3,006.70/oz earlier in the session. Modest U.S. inflation numbers have boosted hopes the Federal Reserve will ease monetary policy, ING analysts say in a note. The market has priced in another 25 basis point reduction in June, with around 70bps of easing expected for the full year, ING writes. Lower interest rates typically boost the appeal of non-interest bearing bullion. Gold's rally has also been fuelled by concerns that tariffs will boost inflation and slow economic growth, prompting investors to search for safe-haven assets, ING adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

2326 ET - U.S. steel and aluminum tariffs affect just 0.2% of Australia's goods exports, and proposed tariffs on pharmaceuticals will likely have a similarly small impact, says Adam Boyton, head of Australian economics at ANZ. Australia's trade deficit with the U.S. makes it an unlikely target for country-specific tariffs, he adds. Still, Australia's exposure to developments in the U.S. isn't necessarily through direct or indirect trade linkages. Rather, it is more likely to come through uncertainty affecting business and consumer confidence, and then business-investment and household-spending decisions, Boyton says. There isn't enough evidence in soft data to suggest downside risks to growth are crystallizing, he adds. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

2258 ET - RBC Capital Markets analysts say they are sanguine on the Australian mining sector. While commodities have been buffeted by tariff threats and soft economic data, fears of a major global slowdown are overdone, the analysts reckon. They revise their commodity forecasts, boosting their price estimates for gold but paring for metallurgical coal and lithium. "We forecast most commodity prices to be flattish/small declines in 2025," with the exception of precious metals, they say. RBC's key stock picks are Fortescue, Sandfire, Pilbara Minerals and IGO as well as Bellevue Gold, Westgold Resources, Regis Resources, South32, Mineral Resources and BHP. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2254 ET - Iron ore rises in early Asian trading. Steel mills remain pessimistic about future market demand, but profits so far have rebounded significantly along with molten-iron production, analysts at Nanhua Futures write in a note. There is little room for further downside in prices, which are already at relatively low levels, and the market remains tight, with iron-ore port inventories destocking beyond seasonal levels and fundamentals staying strong, they say. Further upside will depend on market demand, they add. The most-traded iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is 1.7% higher at 789.0 yuan a ton. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

2242 ET - Palm oil prices fall in Asian trading amid continued slow physical demand, AmInvestment Bank says in a note. Palm oil market is expected to remain range-bound until mid-April before easing, helping to reduce palm oil's unusual premium over soyoil, it says. However, techinical analysis suggest CPO futures appears bullish, prices could hover around MYR4,480/ton-MYR4,570/ton, any price adjustment could offer opportunity for long position, it adds. AmInvestment Bank sees support for CPO prices at 4,430 ringgit/ton and resistance at 4,567 ringgit/ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery falls 22 ringgit to 4,517 ringgit/ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2240 ET - The planned acquisition of Radius Recycling by a U.S. unit of Toyota Tsusho at a roughly 115% premium looks positive for ASX-listed metals recycler Sims, says Citi analyst Paul McTaggart. The Radius deal represents an EV/Ebitda multiple of 7.4X on 2027 estimates and a trailing three-year multiple of 8.3X, based on Visible Alpha consensus, says McTaggart. "We see this as a positive read-across for SGM with SGM trading on FY27 EV/Ebitda of 3.4X," he says. Citi has a neutral rating and A$15.50 target on Sims. The stock is up 4.7% at A$14.45. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2211 ET - Base metals are broadly higher in Asian trading. The three-month LME copper contract rises 0.3% to $9,810.00/ton, while that of nickel is 1.2% higher and aluminum is flat. Investors are gauging market appetite for new highs despite U.S. recession risks in focus amid renewed geopolitical tensions between Russia and Ukraine, the Sucden Financial team writes in a note. Meanwhile, the ongoing trade war is supporting aluminum prices, it adds. The lack of fundamental and technical triggers mean a significant push higher in prices will be challenging, Sucden Financial says. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

2205 ET - Malaysia's equity market investors may remain defensive amid uncertainty over U.S. reciprocal tariff decisions on April 2, CIMB Securities analysts Ivy Ng Lee Fang and Lim Yue Jia say in a note. Large foreign outflows, driven by risk aversion and weak 4Q 2024 corporate earnings, have pressured the KLCI, they note. The index is 8.6% lower year-to-date at 1500.65. Foreign shareholding stands at 19.6% as of end-February, just above the record low of 19.4%, they flag. Catalysts such as easing trade tensions, a stronger ringgit, improved corporate earnings, or government initiatives are needed to drive a rebound, they add. CIMB highlights Genting Malaysia, Sime Darby, Malayan Banking, Gamuda, and RHB Bank as key picks for their large-cap status and high dividend yields. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2159 ET - Chinese shares are higher in early trade, supported by consumer and insurance stocks. Investors await China's February economic data due Monday, including industrial output and retail sales. Gainers in the session include Yonghui Superstores, which is up 1.7%, Kweichow Moutai up 0.9%, China Life Insurance adding 0.6% and Ping An Insurance 1.1% higher. Steel stocks are broadly lower, with Baoshan Iron & Steel down 1.6%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index is 0.3% higher at 3369.64, the Shenzhen Composite Index is up 0.1% and the ChiNext Price Index rises 0.7%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Sector Update: Health Care Stocks Higher Premarket Friday
data.description:
Health care stocks were higher premarket Friday, with the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund up 0.4% and the iShares Biotechnology ETF 0.6% higher recently.

Sutro Biopharma said it appointed Jane Chung as its new chief executive and plans to nearly halve its workforce, following a strategic review of the company's portfolio. Sutro Biopharma shares fell by over 14% pre-bell.

Inovio Pharmaceuticals shares were up more than 3% after the company said interim results from an ongoing phase 1 study of DNA-encoded monoclonal antibodies for COVID-19 demonstrated durable in vivo antibody production.

ANI Pharmaceuticals shares were over 1% higher after the company said the US Food and Drug Administration has approved Iluvien to treat chronic non-infectious uveitis affecting the eye's posterior segment.

data.title: Desktop Metal Inc expected to post a loss of 28 cents a share - Earnings Preview

• Desktop Metal Inc , is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on March 17 (estimated) for the period ending December 31 2024

  • The Burlington Massachusetts-based company is expected to report a 15.8% decrease in revenue to $44.1 million from $52.35 million a year ago, according to the estimate from one analyst, based on LSEG data.

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

The investment trust added that trading of its shares on the London bourse under the new name and AFL ticker will start March 17.

The name change will not impact shareholders' stakes and existing share certificates will remain valid for all purposes.

data.title: Canada Factory Shipments Continue to Rise, Climbing 1.7% in January
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

OTTAWA--Canadian manufacturing activity maintained its run of growth into the new year, with sales in January buoyed by a rebound in motor vehicle trade.

Factory shipments rose 1.7% from the month before to a seasonally adjusted 72.98 billion Canadian dollars, the equivalent of about $50.53 billion, Statistics Canada said Friday.

The result was softer than the data agency's advance estimate for monthly rise of 2.0%, though marks a fourth straight month of gains following an upwardly revised increase of 0.5% in December.

Compared with a year earlier, manufacturing sales in January were up 3.0%.

Sales in constant dollars, or volume terms, were up 0.9% for the month to C$55.02 billion.

Motor vehicle sales jumped 11.1% for the month, the largest month-over-month increase since March 2023, after a drop of 4.7% in December as operations resumed following planned winter shutdowns.

Excluding vehicles, parts and accessories, manufacturing sales were 0.9% higher in January.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: InPlay Oil Q4 Earnings Miss Analysts' Estimates
data.description:
InPlay Oil reported Friday comprehensive earnings in the fourth quarter of 2024 that missed analysts' estimates on lower than expected revenues as production slipped.

The company declared a comprehensive income of $2.2 million, or $0.02 per share, below the analyst consensus earnings forecast of $0.04 per share compiled by FactSet.

The result was down from a comprehensive income of $11.6 million, or $0.13 per share, booked in the year-ago period.

Sales stood at $40.0 million, dropping from $47.6 million and missing the analyst consensus sales estimate of $42.8 million compiled by FactSet.

Production slipped to 9,376 barrels of oil equivalent per day (boe/d) from 9,596 boe/d.

"2024 was a year of disciplined execution, operational efficiency, and delivering shareholder returns," the company said in its message to shareholders. "We remain focused on financial strength, sustainable production, and value creation for our shareholders."

Meanwhile, the company said its organic 2024 capital program without acquisition/disposition activity resulted in proved developed producing reserves of 17.2 million barrels of oil equivalent (boe), total proved reserves of 43.9 million boe, and total proved plus probable reserves of 58.7 million boe.

IPO eased $0.02 to $1.58 yesterday.
